title: QA/specialist agent frontmatter tools: omits plugin MCP tools — code-graph structurally unreachable from QA; live invariant failed with 0 impact_of calls
notes:
COMPLETED: Phase B QA/specialist agent frontmatter MCP-tool parity fix (NOT approved per protocol).

EVIDENCE-BEFORE-FIX TRAIL:

1. ROOT CAUSE STATEMENT (from claude-workflow-plugin-366.5 forensic):
   QA subagents made 0 mcp__plugin_claude-workflow_code-graph__impact_of calls
   in the seed live trace because qa.md's 'tools:' frontmatter line enumerated
   a fixed allowlist with no mcp__* entries — per code.claude.com/docs/en/sub-agents:
   'To restrict tools, use either the tools field (allowlist) or the
   disallowedTools field (denylist). This example uses tools to exclusively
   allow Read, Grep, Glob, and Bash. The subagent can't edit files, write
   files, or use any MCP tools'. The SDK structurally strips MCP tools from
   the subagent's surface when 'tools:' is an allowlist that omits them.
   Same defect in backend.md, frontend.md, devops.md, orchestrator.md.
   Evidence: cassettes/seed/node-react-auth-2026-06-11T23-34-49-784Z.jsonl —
   declared 7 code-graph tools at root scope, 0 invocations from QA, QA's
   free text 'code-graph MCP unavailable' (false from outside QA, true from
   inside QA's stripped surface).

2. DOC QUOTE ON MCP TOOL NAMES (code.claude.com/docs/en/permissions):
   'mcp__puppeteer matches any tool provided by the puppeteer server'
   'mcp__puppeteer__* wildcard syntax that also matches all tools from the puppeteer server'
   'mcp__puppeteer__puppeteer_navigate matches the puppeteer_navigate tool'
   The same naming registry applies to subagent 'tools:' allowlist entries
   (the docs do not show MCP entries in 'tools:' examples but the canonical
   tool-naming registry is shared across the permission system).
   Plugin-bundled MCP tool form (code.claude.com/docs/en/mcp):
   'mcp__plugin_<plugin-name>_<server-name>__<tool-name>'.

3. FRONTMATTER FORM USED:
   Each non-grader agent's 'tools:' line was extended with four server-level
   grants (the cleanest, resilient-to-new-tools form):
     mcp__plugin_claude-workflow_code-graph  (plugin-load prefix, code-graph)
     mcp__plugin_claude-workflow_bd          (plugin-load prefix, bd)
     mcp__code-graph                         (project-scope prefix, code-graph)
     mcp__bd                                 (project-scope prefix, bd)
   Both prefixes are declared because plugin.json and .mcp.json register the
   servers under different prefixes (plugin-load = full plugin-namespaced;
   project-scope = bare server name). The agent files are bundled by
   plugin.json AND committed to the project tree, so either path can be the
   load path depending on how the user installs the plugin.

4. FAILING TEST EVIDENCE (pre-fix run of the new L1 test):
   Created .claude/scripts/tests/agent-mcp-tools-parity.test.sh — asserts
   for every agent file with a 'tools:' frontmatter line that every
   MCP tool referenced in the prompt body is granted in 'tools:'.
   grader.md is exempt-by-design (read-only tools, no MCP needed); the
   exemption is encoded in EXEMPT_AGENTS with a justification.

   PRE-FIX OUTPUT:
   (check_agent) backend.md: body references MCP tool bd_doc_read — tools: lacks grant
   (check_agent) devops.md:  body references MCP tool bd_doc_read — tools: lacks grant
   (check_agent) frontend.md: body references MCP tool bd_doc_read — tools: lacks grant
   (check_agent) orchestrator.md: body references MCP tool impact_of, code_search,
                                  code_context, bd_doc_read, bd_doc_write — tools: lacks grants
   (check_agent) qa.md:      body references MCP tool impact_of, code_search,
                              code_context, bd_doc_read, bd_doc_write — tools: lacks grants
   FAIL: agent-mcp-tools-parity: real repo holds parity (expected 0, actual 1)

   POST-FIX OUTPUT: PASS (5/5 assertions including 4 META-TESTS that prove
   sensitivity in both directions plus the exemption + no-tools-line cases).

5. FIXES SHIPPED (no commit, no QA approve):

   a. AGENT FRONTMATTER (5 files):
      .claude/agents/qa.md, backend.md, frontend.md, devops.md, orchestrator.md
      'tools:' line extended with 4 server-level mcp__ grants each.

   b. QA section 3a + orchestrator section 1a wording tightening:
      Replaced the graceful-degradation prose so an EMPTY index is NOT a
      degradation reason — code_index_health reporting empty/missing means
      'first impact_of call builds the index lazily; PROCEED'. Degrade to
      grep ONLY when the code-graph tools are structurally absent from the
      tool surface (no mcp__*code-graph* entry). Both files updated.

   c. FIXTURE.YAML INVARIANTS BLOCK RESTORED:
      .claude/tests/e2e/fixtures/node-react-auth/fixture.yaml — re-added the
      invariants block stripped during the live run's restore. Includes
      qa-queried-impact-of as the Phase B regression anchor. The failing
      _invariants.unit.spec.ts:845 test ('real fixture.yaml files parse and
      declare invariants for all 6 fixtures') flips green post-fix.

   d. RESTORE EXCLUSION MECHANISM (.claude/tests/e2e/lib/runFixture.ts):
      Added HARNESS_METADATA_FILES = ['fixture.yaml'] and the
      snapshotHarnessMetadata / restoreHarnessMetadata helpers.
      snapshotFixture now captures fixture.yaml's bytes AT THE TOP OF THE
      RUN (before stash) into snapshot.harnessMetadata; restoreFixture now
      writes those bytes back AT THE END of the restore (after reset/clean/
      pop) so operator-authored harness metadata survives the cycle
      regardless of git's intermediate state. This is the smallest safe
      change — additive, narrowly scoped, never overrides agent-writable
      files. Pathspec exclusion (':(exclude)fixture.yaml' with git checkout)
      was considered but rejected: git reset --hard does not accept pathspec;
      switching to per-file restoration would have widened the diff. The
      snapshot-and-rewrite path is the minimal way to preserve the
      operator's edit while keeping the existing reset/clean/pop sequence
      intact.

   e. NEW UNIT TEST .claude/tests/e2e/specs/_fixture-restore.unit.spec.ts:
      4 specs covering (1) operator edit survives the basic cycle, (2)
      fixture WITHOUT a fixture.yaml round-trips cleanly, (3) snapshotFixture
      captures fixture.yaml bytes at snapshot time, (4) restoreFixture
      override is the LAST step and wins against intermediate state.
      Spec 4 is the meta-sensitive test: when the fix is disabled (the
      'if (snapshot.harnessMetadata)' guard is mutated to skip), spec 4
      fails — empirically verified.

   f. docs/AGENTS.md SWEEP:
      Each agent block's 'tools:' line updated to match the canonical
      agent file; added a short MCP-tool-grants rationale block under each.

6. TEST COUNTS:
   make lint: PASS (silent).
   make test (L1):       Total: 11  Passed: 11  Failed: 0 (was 10; +1 new parity test).
   make test-all (L1+L2): L1 Total: 11/11; L2 Specs: 21/21 Assertions: 409/409.
   cd .claude/tests/e2e && npm run test:unit:
     11 test files PASS, 139 tests PASS, 3 skipped (HAVE_TRACE/BD_AVAILABLE
     gates; expected). Includes the newly-passing :845 assertion in
     _invariants.unit.spec.ts AND the 4 new _fixture-restore.unit.spec.ts
     tests.
   node -e plugin.json parse: PASS.

7. LLM OBSERVATIONS:
   - The doc-quote chain matters here: code.claude.com/docs/en/permissions
     explicitly defines the 'mcp__<server>' server-level grant; the
     subagent docs do NOT show an MCP entry in 'tools:' examples but the
     same naming registry applies. Future editors who need to validate
     this empirically can run a live test with one agent declaring just
     'mcp__bd' and watching whether bd_* tool calls succeed.
   - The dual-prefix approach (mcp__plugin_claude-workflow_<server> +
     mcp__<server>) covers both install paths. If the docs ever clarify
     that only one applies per scope, the agent files can be slimmed
     down — but until then, declaring both is the resilient choice.
   - The fixture-restore fix's smallest-safe-change framing matters: the
     snapshot-and-rewrite path is independent of git's stash semantics,
     so any future bug in git stash pop's conflict handling (or a future
     post-edit hook that commits fixture.yaml) cannot wipe the operator's
     bytes again.
   - The Phase B 'qa-queried-impact-of' invariant added to
     node-react-auth/fixture.yaml will not pass on the seed trace
     (0 impact_of calls), but WILL pass on the NEXT live run with the
     new frontmatter — that's the proof-by-future-trace this fix is set
     up to deliver.

NOT APPROVED — gate stays at qa-gate-entered per task brief.

LABELS: qa-gate-entered (preserved); rubric-pending (preserved from enter).

DEPENDS ON
  → claude-workflow-plugin-366.5: forensic root cause + Phase B trace anchor.

title: Dirty-on-entry recovery wipes uncommitted fixture.yaml before snapshotHarnessMetadata captures it
description: Cause B from Run 3 forensic. In runFixture.ts the order is: (1) dirty-on-entry check runs 'git reset --hard HEAD && git clean -fd' at lines 811-818; (2) snapshotFixture runs at line 838 which calls snapshotHarnessMetadata. If the fixture's nested-git HEAD does not contain the operator's intended fixture.yaml (e.g. the outer plugin repo updated fixture.yaml in commit 256e0c1 but the fixture's nested git HEAD = 7207544 'auto-init' predates that), the operator's edit appears as uncommitted relative to the nested HEAD. The dirty recovery wipes it, snapshot captures already-wiped bytes, and the end-of-run restore writes the wiped bytes back. Net: fixture.yaml -28 lines vs outer HEAD on disk, satisfiesInvariants() fails with 'no invariants declared'.
notes:
COMPLETED OFFLINE (no paid runs). Evidence-before-fix protocol followed.

=== ROOT CAUSE (verbatim, with citations) ===

In runFixture.ts (pre-fix), the dirty-on-entry self-heal at lines 811-818 ran BEFORE snapshotFixture at line 838. Sequence in run 3 of Phase B:
  1. Fixture's nested git HEAD = 7207544 ('fixture: initial state (auto-init)', Mon May 11 2026) predates the outer plugin repo's invariants commit 256e0c1 (Fri Jun 12 2026).
  2. Outer repo's fixture.yaml has the invariants block; inner repo sees those 28 lines as UNCOMMITTED relative to its own HEAD.
  3. runFixture sees 'git status --porcelain' dirty → fires recovery: 'git reset --hard HEAD && git clean -fd -e node_modules'.
  4. Reset wipes the operator's intended invariants block. clean wipes crash-leftover untracked files.
  5. snapshotFixture(line 838) calls snapshotHarnessMetadata which now reads the POST-RESET (no-invariants) fixture.yaml bytes.
  6. End-of-run restoreHarnessMetadata writes those post-reset bytes back → working tree ends -28 lines vs outer HEAD.
  7. Mid-run, satisfiesInvariants() reads the file at this state → parseInvariantsFromYaml returns 0 → matcher fails with 'no invariants declared in fixture.yaml'.

Empirical verification offline:
  - Pre-restore working tree (manual git checkout -- fixture.yaml at the start of this session): 28-line diff vs HEAD, matching the brief's claim.
  - Test red baseline: vitest specs/_invariants.unit.spec.ts:845 ('real fixture.yaml files parse and declare invariants for all 6 fixtures') failed with 'fixture node-react-auth should declare invariants (post-0.8): expected 0 to be greater than 0' — proving the working tree fixture.yaml had 0 invariants.

=== CAUSE A (Question A) — REBUTTED ===

The brief hypothesised code-graph tools 'vanished' in run 3. FALSE PREMISE — the trace shows 7 code-graph tools declared identically to run 2 (each occurring 3x: top-level toolsAvailable, systemInit.tools, systemInit.raw.tools). pluginErrors=[], systemInit.mcpServers shows plugin:claude-workflow:code-graph status=connected in both runs. The brief author counted the 2x server-name occurrences (server registration scope only) and mistakenly concluded tool-level names also vanished.

The REAL cause-A finding: QA had impact_of available and chose not to call it (model-behaviour gap). Filed as claude-workflow-plugin-366.9, stays OPEN as it needs a paid run to test mitigations.

=== FIX (366.8) ===

Files changed:
  - .claude/tests/e2e/lib/runFixture.ts: extracted dirty-on-entry recovery into exported selfHealOnEntry(fixturePath) which captures snapshotHarnessMetadata BEFORE the git reset+clean and writes it back IMMEDIATELY after. Replaced the inline lines 811-818 with a call to selfHealOnEntry.
  - .claude/tests/e2e/specs/_fixture-restore.unit.spec.ts: added spec 'Run-3 regression: uncommitted fixture.yaml SURVIVES the dirty-on-entry self-heal that precedes snapshotFixture (claude-workflow-plugin-366.8)' — sets up a sandbox with operator's uncommitted invariants edit + crash leftovers + agent mid-crash mutation, calls selfHealOnEntry, asserts (a) fixture.yaml retains operator bytes, (b) crash leftover is cleaned, (c) tracked src.js reverted to HEAD; then runs the full snapshotFixture / mid-run-mutation / restoreFixture round-trip and re-asserts (d) fixture.yaml ends with operator's bytes.
  - .claude/tests/e2e/specs/_phase-b-run3-trace.unit.spec.ts: new anchor pinning run-3 facts (367-line spec).
  - .claude/tests/e2e/cassettes/seed/node-react-auth-2026-06-12T00-50-56-312Z.jsonl: anchored.
  - .claude/tests/e2e/fixtures/node-react-auth/ (inner git): committed adoption of outer-repo invariants block (commit 401bc13 'fixture: adopt outer-repo invariants block (claude-workflow-plugin-366.8)') — defence in depth: future runs see a clean tree on entry so the dirty-on-entry recovery is exercised less.

Mutation-test verification: temporarily replaced restoreHarnessMetadata(fixturePath, preservedMetadata) with 'void preservedMetadata' in selfHealOnEntry; the new test failed with the EXACT 28-line wipe pattern from run 3 ('invariants:' block missing). Reinstated the call; test green.

Working-tree fixture.yaml restored from HEAD (256e0c1) — outer repo working tree clean re fixture.yaml.

=== INVARIANT VERDICTS (run 3 trace × HEAD fixture.yaml) — VERBATIM ===

stop-requires-approval:        PASS (2 Stop:allow events with qa-approved on auth-86b.1, auth-86b.2, auth-b69)
orchestrator-no-edits:         PASS (no Write/Edit/MultiEdit attributable to orchestrator)
completion-contract:           SKIPPED (documented trace gap — no structured completion payloads)
label-milestones:              FAIL (missing qa-pending — added then removed by qa-approved within the same run; set-membership approximation in beadsLabelTransitions diff)
declared-subagents-only:       PASS (all 7 invocations matched declared or always-allowed roles)
qa-queried-impact-of:          FAIL (0 impact_of calls from QA; fileWrites=8; skip branch did NOT fire because toolsAvailable contains 7 code-graph entries) — NOT the skip the brief expected; QA had access and did not call

=== TESTS RUN (all green) ===
  - cd .claude/tests/e2e && npm run test:unit: 148 pass / 4 skipped / 12 files
  - make lint: silent pass (shellcheck on all hook scripts)
  - make test (L1): 11/11
  - make test-all (L1+L2): 21 specs, 409 assertions, 11/11+21/21 pass

=== run-4 CONFIDENCE ESTIMATE ===

If a paid run-4 were spent: confidence ~50% that qa-queried-impact-of flips green.
Reasoning + remaining unknowns:
  + The 366.8 fix removes the structural cause that masked run 3's verdict (fixture.yaml will arrive at satisfiesInvariants with the invariants block intact, so 6 invariants will evaluate, not 0).
  + The 366.6 frontmatter widening WAS verified live in run 3 (code-graph tools in toolsAvailable) so QA's surface includes impact_of.
  - Run 3 ALREADY had the 366.6 frontmatter fix in effect and QA still did not call impact_of (a model-behaviour gap, not a tooling gap).
  - label-milestones will likely STILL fail (qa-pending continues to be added-then-removed; orthogonal to 366.8).
  - Unknown: whether the orchestrator-as-subagent + double-recovery shape from run 3 is reproducible or model-noise; if reproducible, QA's second invocation context may consistently truncate the impact_of cue.

To raise run-4 confidence above 80%: 366.9 needs a follow-up that strengthens QA's section-3a prompt (literal 'first action when fileWrites>0: call impact_of for each changed symbol') OR adds a soft-warning signal from verify-before-stop.sh when QA approval landed with 0 impact_of calls. Either path is offline-testable before a paid run.

title: QA did not call impact_of despite frontmatter-granted access (Cause A from Run 3)
description:
Run 3 of Phase B (node-react-auth, 2026-06-12T00-50-56-312Z): QA had all 7 code-graph tools in its surface via the 366.6 frontmatter widening (verified: toolsAvailable contains mcp__plugin_claude-workflow_code-graph__impact_of, fileWrites=8, code-graph server status=connected) but made zero impact_of calls anywhere in the run. The qa-queried-impact-of invariant correctly FAILED (skip branch did NOT fire because tools were structurally present).

This is a MODEL-BEHAVIOUR gap, not a structural one. The forensic brief's Question A hypothesised that code-graph tools 'vanished' in run 3 (false premise — all 7 tools were declared identically in run 2 and run 3 traces, 3x each across toolsAvailable / systemInit.tools / systemInit.raw.tools). The real anomaly: QA's prompt as triggered by the Stop-hook block-then-recover path (qa.toolUseId=toolu_01ENVjaNZNNKXvpeiDwaSeDK and toolu_01CuLcXL7PuiPGe5hpsf2Qy5, both root-parented) may not have surfaced the impact_of instruction strongly enough for the model to invoke it.

NOT IN SCOPE OF 366.8: this task tracks the follow-up investigation into either (a) tightening qa.md's section 3a to require an impact_of call as the literal first action when fileWrites > 0, or (b) adding a verify-before-stop.sh signal that surfaces 'QA approved without consulting impact_of' as a soft warning. Either path needs a follow-up paid run to validate.

Evidence anchored in .claude/tests/e2e/specs/_phase-b-run3-trace.unit.spec.ts (negative-fact anchor: impactCalls.length===0).
notes:
CLOSED 2026-06-12 — completed-with-followup.

=== SCOPE COMPLETED ===

Cue shipped to 2 surfaces and verified IN-FILE on the live repo:
  - verify-before-stop.sh lines 977-982 — FIRST: for every changed file ... impact_of (code-graph MCP) ... fold high-fan-in callers ... fall back to grep/code_search; do not silently skip.
  - qa.md section 3a, lines 154/158/160 — FIRST ACTION: Before reading the diff, before running review modules, before writing review notes — run impact_of (code-graph MCP) on each changed exported symbol AND on each whole changed file.

Failing-test-first captured: .claude/scripts/tests/qa-impact-of-cue.test.sh — 4 PRE-FIX failures, all flipped to PASS post-fix. Meta-tests in place.

=== POST-FIX LIVE VALIDATION (run 4) ===

Run 4 (cassettes/seed/node-react-auth-2026-06-12T02-18-12-871Z.jsonl, 2026-06-12, ~$5.46, 1376s, 41 turns, success): satisfiesInvariants evaluated LIVE for the first time (366.8's effect; fixture.yaml preserved end-to-end). Engine verdict on the run-4 trace:
  - 3 PASS (stop-requires-approval, orchestrator-no-edits, declared-subagents-only)
  - 1 SKIP (completion-contract — documented trace gap)
  - 2 FAIL (label-milestones — structural NET-diff bug, see new bug claude-workflow-plugin-9ke; qa-queried-impact-of — 0 impact_of calls AGAIN despite cue shipping)

The fix in this task did NOT move QA's impact_of invocation rate. Root cause: the cue is on the right surfaces (qa.md 3a is in every QA spawn's system prompt; verify-before-stop.sh's heredoc has the cue at the repo level) but the cue does not actually reach QA via the channels we assumed:
  - The Stop-hook recovery template fires ONLY on Stop block-then-recover cycles. In a clean-flow run where the orchestrator delegates to QA proactively (no block needed), the heredoc never fires.
  - For RUN 4 specifically, the Stop hook DID fire (2 blocks), BUT the fixture-local script that ran is the stale render (last touched a8a7bcf 'v3 upgrade + G8 test harness' — predates 8f07ea9 366.9 cue fix). Hooks.json resolves to CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/.claude/scripts/verify-before-stop.sh which is the fixture-rendered (stale) copy, not the repo copy. Cue absent from both block reasons (anchored in _phase-b-run4-trace.unit.spec.ts).
  - qa.md 3a IS in every QA spawn's system prompt. 4 live runs (run 1/2/3/4) all show 0 impact_of calls. In-system-prompt prose alone is empirically insufficient.

=== SUPERSEDED BY ===

New standalone bug **claude-workflow-plugin-n6d** (p1) — 'impact_of cue does not reach QA on clean-flow runs ... qa.md 3a alone empirically insufficient (4 live runs, 0 calls)'. Carries the cue-channel problem forward past Phase B with the mechanical option (orchestrator pre-computes impact_of and embeds results in the QA delegation packet) added to the design menu inherited from this task.

Closing as completed-with-followup: the cue ships, the failing-test-first L1 captures it, the gap is now in a separate bug with explicit run-4 citations. Phase B's QA mandate (cue present in code) is satisfied; QA mandate (cue effective) is in the follow-up bug.
close_reason: Completed-with-followup. Cue shipped to 2 surfaces (verify-before-stop.sh + qa.md 3a) per spec; verified in-file. Live run-4 demonstrated the cue is in the wrong delivery channels (fixture-stale Stop-hook copy lacked the cue; qa.md 3a alone empirically insufficient over 4 live runs). Superseded by new standalone bug claude-workflow-plugin-n6d with run-4 evidence + the mechanical 'orchestrator pre-computes impact_of' design option.

title: F2/F4/F5/F7/F8 ignore COMMAND_EXCLUSIONS; fault-classes.md claim is false
description:
Phase C.1 mutation generator: only F1, F3, F6 invoke should_skip(), which is the function that enforces COMMAND_EXCLUSIONS. F2 (guard deletion), F4 (variable-default removal), F5 (pipeline-segment drop), F7 (sentinel mutation), and F8 (arithmetic) all read the target file line-by-line WITHOUT checking should_skip. fault-classes.md lines 232-235 claim 'The generator pattern list explicitly skips any line whose first token is in the exclusion set (mutation.conf key command_exclusions).' This is materially false for 5/8 fault classes.

Currently latent — no destructive-command-with-mutatable-shape lines exist in .claude/scripts/*.sh. Becomes exploitable the moment a target with such a line is added or mutated manually.

Mutants apply inside a throwaway worktree (good) but the test command runs with the user's environment (inheriting $HOME, $SAFE_BASE, etc.). A mutant of `rm -rf "${SAFE_BASE:-/tmp/safe}"` becomes `rm -rf "${SAFE_BASE}"` and IF that env var is set to the user's home in the parent shell, the mutant deletes $HOME despite the worktree containment.

Fix: add `should_skip "$line" && continue` to gen_f2, gen_f4, gen_f5, gen_f7, gen_f8 — symmetric with F1/F3/F6. Add an L1 assertion: for each fault class, build a fixture with an rm-shaped trigger line and assert the generator emits zero mutants.

Proof in QA notes:

  PROBE target:
    rm -rf "${HOME:-/}"            (F4 trigger)
    rm -rf "${SAFE_BASE:-/tmp/safe-default-dir}"  (F4 trigger)
    rm -f file | grep foo | wc -l    (F5 trigger)
    rm -rf qa-approved.lock          (F7 trigger)
    rm -f "$((COUNTER + 1)).log"    (F8 trigger)

  Actual generator output:
    F4 emitted 3 mutants (all 'rm -rf ...' lines)
    F5 emitted 1 mutant (drops 'grep foo' segment)
    F7 emitted 1 mutant ('qa-approved' -> 'qa-approve')
    F8 emitted 1 mutant ('+ 1' -> '+ 2')

title: Bug: bd-github-link.sh hook regex misses canonical 'bd update <tid> --status closed' form
description:
The hook-mode regex on line 357-359 of bd-github-link.sh fails to detect the most natural close-task command. The regex is: 'bd[[:space:]]+update[[:space:]]+([A-Za-z0-9._-]+)[[:space:]]+(.+--status[[:space:]]+closed)'. The '.+' requires at least one character between the task id and '--status', so commands like 'bd update <tid> --status closed' (where there's only a single space) DON'T match. Verified by exhaustive shape testing:
- 'bd update foo --status closed': MISS
- 'bd update foo --status closed --notes bar': matches but BASH_REMATCH[1] captures 'foo --status closed' (incl. spaces), and the id-shape regex on line 365 then rejects it
- 'bd update --status closed foo' (alt order): MISS for same reason
- 'bd close foo': OK
- 'bd update foo -n notes --status closed': OK

The plugin's OWN verify-before-stop.sh uses the canonical form 'bd update "$CURRENT_TASK" --status closed' (lines 435, 742). When the orchestrator/QA agent later runs the same form via the Bash tool, the PostToolUse hook will silently fail to fire the gh-link comment.

SUGGESTED FIX: change '.+' to '.*' in both lines 357 and 358, AND fix the BASH_REMATCH[1] capture-group ambiguity (the alt-order regex captures the rest as group 1 and the tid as group 2, but the code reads BASH_REMATCH[1] unconditionally). Cleanest fix: a single regex with a named capture group, or a 4-way explicit if/elif checking each shape and assigning TASK_TO_CLOSE explicitly. SEVERITY: HIGH — breaks the I3 round-trip in the most common close path.
notes: FIXED: replaced the brittle three-alt regex (lines 357-359) with extract_tid_from_tail() — a token-walking helper that tolerates flag-before-id, --status closed (space) AND --status=closed (equals), multi-id (first wins), surrounding quotes, and shell redirections. The earlier regex's '.+' between tid and --status closed rejected the canonical single-space form used by verify-before-stop.sh:435 and :742. Verified by 10 assertions in .claude/scripts/tests/bd-github-link.test.sh Section 1 covering every shape the plugin emits plus negative cases (--status open, unrelated commands).

title: runFixture must set allowDangerouslySkipPermissions: true alongside permissionMode: bypassPermissions
description: QA review of G8 Phase A (claude-workflow-plugin-0wk.1) found that lib/runFixture.ts line 585 sets permissionMode: 'bypassPermissions' but does NOT pass allowDangerouslySkipPermissions: true in queryOpts. Per the @anthropic-ai/claude-agent-sdk 0.2.138 d.ts (lines 1499 and 1512) and the public docs at code.claude.com/docs/en/agent-sdk/typescript, the SDK contract is 'allowDangerouslySkipPermissions is required when using permissionMode: bypassPermissions'. In the installed 0.2.138 sdk.mjs the flag is read with default false and forwarded to the underlying claude-code executable; whether the CLI hard-rejects bypassPermissions without the flag depends on the binary version. This must be fixed BEFORE the Phase A live capture is attempted, otherwise the live happy-path spec may fail with a permission-mode contract error rather than executing the orchestrator -> backend + frontend -> QA flow. Fix: in runFixture.ts queryOpts (line 578-589), add allowDangerouslySkipPermissions: opts.permissionMode === 'bypassPermissions' || (opts.permissionMode ?? 'bypassPermissions') === 'bypassPermissions' ? true : undefined  (or equivalent). Add a unit test asserting the flag is passed through. Verified in QA review of claude-workflow-plugin-0wk.1.
notes: Added allowDangerouslySkipPermissions: true at runFixture.ts:586; SDK contract satisfied; type check + 22-pass test suite still green
close_reason: fixed: flag pair now matches SDK 0.2.138 contract

title: Bug: bd-github-link.sh pr-create regex misses URL form references
description: The regex in bd-github-link.sh (--manual pr-create mode line 320-322 and hook mode line 416) only matches `Closes #N` or `Closes owner/repo#N` — it does NOT match URL form like `Closes https://github.com/x/y/issues/3`. The Phase 6b acceptance criteria explicitly require all three formats: '#42', 'owner/repo#7', 'https://github.com/x/y/issues/3'. The normalize_ref helper does support URLs (line 119-123) but the outer regex never feeds a URL into BASH_REMATCH. find_gh_link works correctly because it captures everything after gh-link: and feeds the whole thing to normalize_ref. Test: bash .claude/scripts/bd-github-link.sh --manual pr-create body.md where body.md has 'Resolves https://github.com/x/y/issues/3' — no gh-link is appended. SUGGESTED FIX: extend the regex to: ([Cc]loses|[Ff]ixes|[Rr]esolves)[[:space:]]+(https?://github\.com/[A-Za-z0-9_.-]+/[A-Za-z0-9_.-]+/(issues|pull)/[0-9]+|[A-Za-z0-9_.-]+/[A-Za-z0-9_.-]+#[0-9]+|#[0-9]+) on lines 320, 321, 322, and 416. SEVERITY: HIGH — explicitly part of Phase 6b's acceptance criteria.
notes: FIXED: extended pr-create regex on lines 320 (manual mode) and 416 (hook mode) to accept all four ref forms — '#N', 'owner/repo#N', 'https://.../issues/N', 'https://.../pull/N'. Used a single-alternation regex so BASH_REMATCH[2] always carries the ref regardless of form. BASH_REMATCH[3] holds the (issues|pull) sub-capture for URL forms (Bash ERE has no non-capturing groups; we just ignore it). Verified by 5 assertions in .claude/scripts/tests/bd-github-link.test.sh Section 2.

title: Hardening: SHA-pin GitHub Actions in .github/workflows/test.yml
description:
Phase F deferred. Replace tag-pinned actions (@v4) with SHA-pinned references across .github/workflows/test.yml:

title: Mutation sweep does not halt on SIGINT when slow test in flight; cleanup still correct
description:
Phase C.1 mutation harness usability finding (not a containment safety defect).

Behaviour: when the per-mutant test command is slow (e.g. sleep 30, an infinite-loop mutant), pressing Ctrl+C (SIGINT) on the sweep PID does NOT cause it to exit. The sweep continues processing all remaining mutants. The trap fires at script exit (via EXIT), and worktree cleanup IS correct — both the .claude/.mutation-worktrees/ dir and the git worktree registry end up clean.

Root cause: bash defers SIGINT delivery while waiting on a child process. The mutation-sweep's main for-loop calls run_mutant per mutant, which runs the test in a (subshell). When SIGINT arrives during the subshell wait, bash queues it; once the subshell returns, the INT trap fires (which is the same handler as EXIT — cleanup_worktrees), but then control returns to the for-loop because the trap handler did not exit. Loop continues.

Probe evidence (all timestamps UTC):
- t=0: sweep launched with --test-cmd 'sleep 30' --max-mutants 3
- t=3s: worktree m-...-1 visible
- t=5s: SIGINT sent
- t=35s: trap fired once (wt=1); but loop continued — second worktree created
- t=65s: trap fired again (wt=2)
- t=95s: sweep exited naturally after all 3 mutants done; final trap with wt=3

Containment guarantees HOLD: trap runs at exit, no leak. Main tree intact (HEAD + tracked file hashes unchanged across the run).

Fix: in run_mutant or the wrapping for-loop, after each iteration, check if SIGINT has been received and exit early. Bash-idiomatic approach:

  INT_FLAG=0
  trap 'INT_FLAG=1; cleanup_worktrees' INT
  trap 'cleanup_worktrees' EXIT TERM
  ...
  while IFS= read -r line; do
      [ "$INT_FLAG" = "1" ] && break
      ...
  done

User-facing impact: a developer pressing Ctrl+C expects the sweep to stop NOW. Currently they have to wait for all remaining mutants. With a 60-mutant cap and a 60s per-mutant timeout, that's up to an hour of unwanted work after Ctrl+C. Not a safety issue, but the spec brief asked us to probe SIGINT behavior and this is what we found.

title: label-milestones invariant structurally cannot pass on approve-flow runs: trace beadsLabelTransitions is a NET diff so transient labels (qa-pending added then removed in-run) are invisible; refine trace capture to record transition EVENTS (e.g. derive from qa-gate.sh tool calls in the stream) or redefine the invariant (qa-approved implies the gate cycle)
notes:
OPEN.

=== FINDING ===

The label-milestones invariant cannot pass on any approve-flow run, structurally — not because the workflow fails the milestone-sequence contract, but because trace.beadsLabelTransitions captures a NET diff (added-minus-removed per task), so labels that are added and then removed within the same run are invisible. qa-pending is the canonical case: every approve-flow adds it (when creating the task) and removes it (when QA approves). Net diff shows neither.

=== EVIDENCE ===

Run-4 trace beadsLabelTransitions (cassettes/seed/node-react-auth-2026-06-12T02-18-12-871Z.jsonl):
  auth-aug   added=[devops]                                  removed=[]
  auth-neb   added=[qa-approved]                             removed=[]
  auth-neb.1 added=[backend, qa-approved]                    removed=[]
  auth-neb.2 added=[frontend, qa-approved]                   removed=[]

Engine verdict (verbatim from evaluateAll on the trace + the run-4-anchored fixture.yaml):
  [FAIL] label-milestones: missing milestone label add(s): [qa-pending] — observed adds across run: [backend, devops, frontend, qa-approved]

But qa-pending WAS demonstrably cycled, multiple times — toolCalls in the same trace include:
  - 'bd --no-daemon label add auth-neb.1 qa-pending'
  - 'bash .../qa-gate.sh enter auth-neb.1' (3+ calls)
  - 'bash .../qa-gate.sh approve auth-neb.1' (which atomically removes qa-pending)
  - identical sequence for auth-neb.2 and auth-neb
  - manual 'bd --no-daemon label remove auth-neb.1 qa-pending' workaround calls (because the qa-gate.sh helper was failing under the bd-daemon recursion bug)

The run cycled the gate correctly across 3 tasks (auth-neb.1, auth-neb.2, auth-neb). Run 3 (cassettes/seed/node-react-auth-2026-06-12T00-50-56-312Z.jsonl) showed the identical failure on a different epic shape (auth-86b lineage). So the invariant has now failed identically across 2 separate live runs on demonstrably correct workflows — that's the structural-cannot-pass evidence.

=== TWO REPAIR OPTIONS ===

Option 1 — refine trace capture to record transition EVENTS (not net diffs):
  - Extend the trace collector to record every label add/remove as an event in a beadsLabelEvents[] field (timestamp, task, op, label).
  - Keep beadsLabelTransitions as-is for backward compatibility.
  - Rewrite the label-milestones invariant to scan beadsLabelEvents for the full milestone sequence (presence of the add events in declared order, regardless of subsequent removes).
  - This is the 'correct' fix: it captures the actual gate-cycle dynamics and lets the invariant assert the contract it's named after.
  - Cost: a small change to the collector + invariant.
  - Derivation source for the events: either intercept the bd CLI's stdout (parse 'Added label X to issue Y' lines), or harvest from the toolCall stream by recognising 'bd ... label add/remove ...' Bash command shapes plus qa-gate.sh approve/block invocations (whose side effects we know).

Option 2 — redefine the invariant: weaken to 'final-state implies cycle' (qa-approved present ⟹ qa-pending was once added):
  - Trivial; doesn't capture intermediate milestones if any are added (currently only qa-pending → qa-approved exists in the fixtures, so this is fine TODAY).
  - Honest about what the trace can observe.
  - Loses signal for any future milestone (e.g. qa-blocked → qa-pending re-entry in a block-then-fix cycle would be invisible).

Recommendation: Option 1. It costs a bounded amount of capture code and unblocks any future milestone-progression invariant (J21 escalation states, block-then-recover handshakes). Option 2 is a tactical workaround that hides the cause.

=== HANDOFF ===

Standalone bug (no parent epic). Carries past Phase B. P2 because:
  - It does not affect production workflows; the gate works correctly.
  - It only affects the in-test invariant evaluator and our ability to assert milestone progression.
  - It cannot be fixed by tightening the workflow; the fix is in the trace capture layer or the invariant definition layer.

Evidence anchored: _phase-b-run4-trace.unit.spec.ts (pin on the verbatim engine failure string, run 4) and _phase-b-run3-trace.unit.spec.ts (same failure on run 3 — confirms 'structurally cannot pass on approve-flow runs', not 'run-4 happens to have a coincidence').

title: Bug: docs/MCP_SERVERS.md line 26 references non-existent fixture symbol 'auth-handler' and non-existent invariant 'qa-queried-impact-of-for-every-changed-symbol'
description: QA-of-QA discovered during gate of claude-workflow-plugin-366.2 (Phase B integration). docs/MCP_SERVERS.md paragraph at line 26 fabricates a 'Concrete example': claims the node-react-auth fixture calls code_context({symbol: 'auth-handler'}) and impact_of({symbol: 'auth-handler'}) before creating subtasks AND claims a fixture-declared invariant named 'qa-queried-impact-of-for-every-changed-symbol' asserts QA queried impact_of for every changed symbol. Reality: the node-react-auth fixture has no 'auth-handler' symbol in its seed code (server/index.js, client/), and the invariant does not exist in fixture.yaml or anywhere in .claude/tests/e2e/. Grep zero hits. Fix: replace the paragraph with either (a) an honest aspirational example explicitly framed as 'when wired up' or (b) cite an invariant that actually exists today (e.g., declared-subagents-only, label-milestones), or (c) implement the invariant if it's in the plan. Scope: doc-only; no behavior impact. Discovered-from: claude-workflow-plugin-366.2.
notes:
COMPLETED:
- Implemented invariant `qa-queried-impact-of` in `.claude/tests/e2e/lib/invariants.ts` (invariant #6).
- Registered the new name in the INVARIANTS registry map.
- Added the invariant to `.claude/tests/e2e/fixtures/node-react-auth/fixture.yaml` (verification-suite Phase B target).
- Added 12 new unit tests in `_invariants.unit.spec.ts`: positive, plugin-qualifier-tolerance, bare-name tolerance, min_calls param, code-graph-absent skip, no-fileWrites skip, evaluateAll skip aggregation, META-TEST stripping QA impact_of call, META-TEST orchestrator-attributed call, META-TEST backend-attributed call, no-QA-subagent failure path.
- Rewrote the fabricated Concrete-example paragraph in `docs/MCP_SERVERS.md` to reference the REAL invariant name (`qa-queried-impact-of`) and a REAL symbol from the node-react-auth fixture (`createApp`, the Express app factory in `server/index.js`).
- Updated stale MCP table line in `fixtures/rubric-revision-loop/.claude/skills/workflow-engine/SKILL.md` from `code-context-mcp` to `code-graph-mcp` with the full 7-tool surface.

KEY DECISIONS:
- Approximation: 'every changed symbol' is NOT verifiable from the Trace schema (no diff-symbol-set field), so the implemented form is 'QA made ≥1 impact_of call when fileWrites is non-empty'. Documented in the docstring with a precedent reference to label-milestones' set-membership-in-lieu-of-ordering. min_calls param defaults to 1; a Trace-schema extension is the upgrade path.
- Two skip branches, both with documented reasons: (a) code-graph absent from toolsAvailable (server not loaded, agents degrade to search-only flow by design) and (b) fileWrites empty (no diff -> contract vacuous).
- Tool-name pattern uses `/code-graph.*impact_of|^impact_of$/` to tolerate the SDK's plugin-qualifier rewriting (`mcp__plugin_claude-workflow_code-graph__impact_of`) AND the bare in-process server tool name.
- Plugin-qualifier-tolerant QA subagent matching reuses the existing `stripQualifier` helper.
- Invariant added ONLY to node-react-auth (the Phase B live-validation target). Other fixtures' flows predate the code-graph wiring; adding it there would generate spurious failures or skips.

VERIFICATION:
- `npx tsc --noEmit`: only the pre-existing `_lib.unit.spec.ts:136` error (confirmed via git stash); no new TS errors.
- `npm run test:unit`: 125 passed | 2 skipped (127 total); _invariants.unit.spec.ts went from 43 -> 55 tests (12 new).
- `make lint`: OK.
- `make test-all`: Specs 20/20 pass, Assertions 392/392 pass.
- `grep -n 'auth-handler|qa-queried-impact-of-for-every' docs/ -r`: zero hits (fabrications gone).
- `grep -rn 'qa-queried-impact-of'` lib/invariants.ts + fixture.yaml + MCP_SERVERS.md: all three reference the real name.

IN PROGRESS: None — ready for QA-of-QA review. Per orchestrator brief: not approving, not committing, not pushing, no live runs.

title: Phase C.2 partial-ship in C.1 PR: judge.md registered without orchestrator wiring
description:
C.1 task (claude-workflow-plugin-n45.1) ships several Phase C.2 artifacts that the task description explicitly excluded ('C.1 only ships the seam; the LLM judge is C.2'):

Files that are C.2 deliverables shipped in C.1:
- .claude/agents/judge.md (new — full judge subagent prompt)
- .claude/tests/mutation/judge-gate.sh (new — precision-gate)
- .claude/tests/mutation/calibration/calibration-set.json (new)
- .claude/scripts/tests/judge-calibration.test.sh (new — 65 assertions, all pass)
- .claude/scripts/workflow-model-apply.sh (modified to track judge.md)
- .claude/scripts/tests/agent-mcp-tools-parity.test.sh (modified to exempt judge)
- .claude/scripts/tests/no-nested-spawn-instructions.test.sh (modified to check judge)
- plugin.json agents[] (now includes ./.claude/agents/judge.md)

Internally consistent — every test passes. The mismatch with task contract:
- The task description says: 'What this script does NOT do (those are C.2/C.3): call the LLM judge directly. C.1 only ships the seam.'
- The 'IN PROGRESS: None — ready for QA' summary lists C.1 deliverables only; judge.md and judge-gate.sh are absent from the list.

Critical downstream gap: judge.md is REGISTERED in plugin.json agents[] but the orchestrator has no JUDGE-RELAY section (only RUBRIC-RELAY for the Phase A grader). The orchestrator does not know to spawn the judge subagent. The plugin advertises a capability the workflow cannot deliver.

Decisions to make:
1. Either roll back the C.2 artifacts to a separate C.2 task/PR (preserve per-phase closeout discipline per spec principle 11), OR
2. Update task n45.1's title/description to encompass C.1 + partial C.2, AND add the JUDGE-RELAY section to orchestrator.md so the registered judge subagent is actually reachable.

Option 2 is structurally simpler if the team accepts the scope expansion.

title: 0.2 Make the QA-gate escalation cap binding
description: Live transcript showed ESCALATION: Iteration 7 (>= 3) still looping with full-suite re-runs. Add escalated state: qa-escalated label + J21 comment at first cap hit, skip-suite while escalated, auto-defer (qa-deferred) on next choiceless Stop, deferred=allow in verify-before-stop, SessionStart surfaces deferred, runner-vs-assertion failure classification. Spec 0.2.
notes:
IN PROGRESS: spec 0.2 implementation landed locally — escalation cap binding now in place. Awaiting QA review.

KEY DELIVERABLES:
- qa-gate.sh gains `choose <approve|continue|tech-debt|defer> <task-id> <note>` subcommand recording a J21 decision (comment + label/counter side effects) — keeps the audit trail thin.
- qa-gate.sh enter clears qa-deferred/qa-escalated + wipes per-iteration cache on every enter so a deferred task naturally resumes normal gating.
- wipe_iteration_state wipes new escalation cache files (last-test-rc, last-failed-checks, last-runner, escalation-posted).
- verify-before-stop.sh: new state machine.
  * Reads qa-deferred → allow Stop (single audited escape valve, principle 6).
  * Reads qa-escalated; at ITER > MAX_ITERATIONS + 1 → auto-defer (qa-deferred label + audit comment + allow).
  * While qa-escalated set, skip suite re-run; reuse cached FAILED_CHECKS + runner.
  * At first cap hit (ITER reaches MAX_ITERATIONS), set qa-escalated + post J21 ESCALATED comment exactly once (idempotent via marker file).
  * Failure classification: classify_test_failure() distinguishes runner (exit 126/127, "command not found", "Cannot find module", testcontainers TypeError, etc.) from assertion. Runner path leads with "Test suite failed to run (environment/runner issue — fix the environment before changing code)".
- session-start.sh: surfaces qa-deferred tasks ABOVE qa-pending with a banner naming the auto-defer flow.

DOCS:
- docs/WORKFLOW.md label table gained qa-escalated, qa-deferred, qa-gate-entered, qa-blocked (full lifecycle).
- docs/HOOKS.md got a new "Escalation State Machine (spec 0.2)" subsection plus updated qa-gate.sh entry mentioning the `choose` subcommand.

TESTS (all green):
- L1: .claude/scripts/tests/qa-gate-choose.test.sh — 30 assertions covering usage errors, all four choose branches, and re-enter clearing qa-deferred/qa-escalated.
- L2: .claude/tests/component/specs/escalation-binding.sh — 42 assertions:
  * Iterations 1-3 invoke the test shim (count climbs to 3) and iter 3 sets qa-escalated + posts one comment.
  * Iter 4 blocks with escalation wording WITHOUT re-running the suite (shim count stays at 3).
  * Iter 5 auto-defers (qa-deferred set, {} envelope, qa-pending preserved).
  * Subsequent Stop with qa-deferred allows immediately.
  * Re-enter clears qa-deferred/qa-escalated and the next Stop re-runs the suite (count to 4).
  * Runner-vs-assertion classification: exit 127 lands on "Test suite failed to run"; exit 1 with assertion output lands on "Tests failing".
  * Counter reset on active-task change: TID2/TID3 hold independent counter files.
  * ACCEPTANCE invariants: suite NOT re-run after cap (npm=3 across iters 4+5) AND qa-deferred recorded by iter 5.
  * META-TEST: mutating bump_iteration to always return 1 makes the cap-assertion fail (qa-escalated never set, npm count exceeds 3).

VERIFICATION:
- make lint  -> clean (shellcheck 0.11.x).
- make test  -> Total: 3 Passed: 3 Failed: 0 (L1).
- make test-all  -> Specs: 17 / Passed: 17 / Failed: 0; Assertions: 295/0.
- L2 escalation-binding.sh standalone summary: Assertions: Passed: 42 Failed: 0.

KEY DECISIONS:
- Auto-defer threshold: ITER > MAX_ITERATIONS + 1 (i.e., ITER >= MAX+2). Iter 3 = cap-hit (block + escalate). Iter 4 = one more escalated Stop (block, no suite re-run). Iter 5 = auto-defer. Matches the prompt's L2 walkthrough exactly.
- The `choose` subcommand is single-shot: approve delegates to cmd_approve, continue resets state, tech-debt calls tech-debt.sh add --bd-task, defer sets qa-deferred. Comments + labels are the record (principle 7).
- Per-task escalation cache files live alongside iteration-count.<task>. Naming: last-test-rc.<task>, last-failed-checks.<task>, last-runner.<task>, escalation-posted.<task> (idempotency marker). All wiped by wipe_iteration_state.
- classify_test_failure() is conservative: only patterns that are unambiguous runner-infra signals classify as runner; everything else stays "assertion" so we never mis-direct an assertion failure to "fix the environment".

FILES CHANGED:
- .claude/scripts/qa-gate.sh
- .claude/scripts/verify-before-stop.sh
- .claude/scripts/session-start.sh
- docs/WORKFLOW.md
- docs/HOOKS.md

FILES CREATED:
- .claude/scripts/tests/qa-gate-choose.test.sh
- .claude/tests/component/specs/escalation-binding.sh

NEW SUBCOMMANDS:
- qa-gate.sh choose <approve|continue|tech-debt|defer> <task-id> <note> [tech-debt: severity file:line effort]

LLM OBSERVATIONS:
- Spec text was internally inconsistent about auto-defer timing: high-level spec said "one more Stop" (iter 4), but the prompt's L2 walkthrough explicitly assigned iter 4 to "blocks with J21 wording" and iter 5 to "auto-defers". Followed the prompt's explicit walkthrough since it is the testable acceptance — iter 4 provides a warning shot, iter 5 acts.
- The escalation comment is idempotent via a marker file (escalation-posted.<task>) rather than a label check, because the label may be removed by `choose continue` and re-set on a future regression — the marker is part of wipe_iteration_state and therefore also clears at the same transitions, but it lives in the filesystem rather than Beads so we don't double up.
- Considered using the existing `qa-blocked` label to mark cap-hit but the spec wants the lifecycle preserved (qa-blocked is the human-pushed "QA found issues" state, distinct from the auto-set qa-escalated). Kept them separate so the audit reads cleanly.
- The `cmd_choose` uses `case` rather than dispatching to four sub-functions to keep audit-comment writing centralised — every choose path emits the same "QA-GATE CHOICE <choice>" prefix so a grep on `bd show` will surface all four.

NEXT STEP: QA review. Did NOT approve the gate; did NOT commit.

comments.author: username
comments.text:
DOCS VERIFICATION (devops, 2026-06-11):

Enumeration mechanism (authoritative):
- API: GET https://api.anthropic.com/v1/models with header 'X-Api-Key: $ANTHROPIC_API_KEY' and 'anthropic-version: 2023-06-01'.
- Source: https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/models-list
- Pagination: cursor-based via after_id/before_id, limit max 1000. 'Has_more' flag + last_id usable for follow-up.
- Response shape per model: { id (e.g. 'claude-opus-4-6'), display_name, created_at (RFC3339, descending — 'more recently released models are listed first'), type:'model', max_input_tokens (context window in tokens), max_tokens, capabilities.{effort:{low,medium,high,xhigh,max,supported},thinking:{...},...} }.
- No CLI listing (verified: 'claude --help' offers --model but no list subcommand).
- No publicly documented 'claude-opus-latest' style alias on the API response (only model-id versions).

Aliases in agent frontmatter and model: settings:
- Source: https://code.claude.com/docs/en/model-config
- Subagent .md 'model:' frontmatter accepts: 'sonnet','opus','haiku','fable','inherit', or a full model ID (e.g. 'claude-opus-4-8'). Source: https://code.claude.com/docs/en/sub-agents (Supported frontmatter fields table).
- settings.json top-level 'model:' field accepts the same — plus 'default','best','opusplan' and the '[1m]' suffix for 1M context window variants (sonnet[1m], opus[1m], claude-opus-4-8[1m]).
- 'best' alias = Fable 5 where org has access, else latest Opus.

Implementation choice:
- Enumerate via curl GET /v1/models when ANTHROPIC_API_KEY is set; cache 1h; rank via .claude/model-ranking (family order, version DESC, unknown-newer = same prefix higher version); prefer the '[1m]' variant where the listing distinguishes context window via max_input_tokens (we'll filter family-prefix matches and pick max max_input_tokens, suffixed with [1m] when supported).
- Fail-open paths: no ANTHROPIC_API_KEY -> warn + keep pin; curl failure / timeout -> warn + keep pin; ranking empty -> warn + keep pin.

title: 0.4 Maximum effort and high time budget
description: Highest supported effort level in settings.json (verify knob name in docs; fail open), keep MAX_THINKING_TOKENS max, shared time-budget block in all six agent prompts per principle 3. Spec 0.4.
notes:

Files updated:
- .claude/settings.json — added top-level 'effortLevel':'xhigh' (the documented per-settings field; 'max' is session-only and not accepted here) AND env.CLAUDE_CODE_EFFORT_LEVEL='max' (env var takes precedence and DOES accept 'max'). Kept MAX_THINKING_TOKENS=64000 since 64000 is the current documented max for fixed-thinking-budget mode (Opus 4.6 / Sonnet 4.6); adaptive-reasoning models (Opus 4.7+, Fable 5) ignore it.
- .claude/agents/{orchestrator,qa,backend,frontend,devops}.md — added 'effort: max' to frontmatter and inserted the shared time-budget block ('Time budget is high. Take the time the task needs; gather context exhaustively...Depth beats speed in every trade. Use generous timeouts on long-running commands.') after each agent's 'Use extended thinking' line.

- CLAUDE_CODE_EFFORT_LEVEL env var documented in https://code.claude.com/docs/en/env-vars — accepts low/medium/high/xhigh/max/auto; takes precedence over /effort and effortLevel setting; persists via env var even for 'max'.

- Both settings.json 'effortLevel':'xhigh' AND env.CLAUDE_CODE_EFFORT_LEVEL='max' are set. The env var wins per docs ('max' deepest reasoning, no token constraint); effortLevel is the durable fallback if the env var is unset by a wrapper.

comments.author: username
comments.text:
DOCS VERIFICATION (devops, 2026-06-11):

Effort knob (authoritative):
- Settings field: 'effortLevel' (top-level in settings.json). Accepts 'low','medium','high','xhigh'. Source: https://code.claude.com/docs/en/settings.
- Env var: 'CLAUDE_CODE_EFFORT_LEVEL' — accepts 'low','medium','high','xhigh','max','auto'. Takes precedence over /effort and effortLevel. Source: https://code.claude.com/docs/en/env-vars.
- 'max' and 'ultracode' are session-only (not accepted in 'effortLevel' or in env var per docs — wait, env var DOES accept 'max'). Recheck: env-vars doc says 'low,medium,high,xhigh,max,auto'. So 'max' via env var is valid AND persists across sessions. Per-agent: subagent frontmatter supports 'effort:' field — overrides session level. Source: https://code.claude.com/docs/en/sub-agents (Supported frontmatter fields table).
- Defaults per model: Fable 5 default 'high'; Opus 4.8 default 'high'; Opus 4.7 default 'xhigh'; Opus 4.6 & Sonnet 4.6 default 'high'. Available levels depend on model (effort capability is exposed in /v1/models response under capabilities.effort.{low,medium,high,xhigh,max}.supported).

MAX_THINKING_TOKENS:
- Still documented. Behavior: setting to 0 disables thinking on Anthropic API except on Fable 5. Other values apply only with fixed-thinking-budget mode (Opus 4.6 and Sonnet 4.6 with CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_ADAPTIVE_THINKING=1). Opus 4.7+ and Fable always use adaptive reasoning.
- Implication: MAX_THINKING_TOKENS=64000 is effectively a no-op on adaptive-reasoning models (Opus 4.7+, Fable 5). We keep it as-is (no documented higher 'max') and add effortLevel/CLAUDE_CODE_EFFORT_LEVEL as the real lever.

Implementation choice:
- settings.json: add top-level 'effortLevel':'xhigh' AND env.CLAUDE_CODE_EFFORT_LEVEL='max' (env wins per docs; 'max' gives deepest reasoning, no constraint on tokens; the per-model max effort fallback handles models that don't support it). Keep MAX_THINKING_TOKENS=64000 and add a one-line comment noting it's adaptive-superseded on newer models but kept as a floor for the Opus 4.6/Sonnet 4.6 fixed-budget path.
- Per-agent frontmatter: add 'effort: max' to all five agents — docs confirm subagent frontmatter accepts 'effort' field and it overrides session level (except CLAUDE_CODE_EFFORT_LEVEL env which still wins). Belt and braces.

Time-budget block: shared text inserted into each of the 5 agent prompts; spec language preserved; note grader.md (Phase A) gets the same block when created.

title: 0.7 Lessons ledger
description: lessons.sh add '<lesson>' --source <task-id> dedup-appends to LESSONS.md. CLAUDE.md pointer; orchestrator reads during planning; QA epic-close proposes lessons via lessons.sh. Seed with the two production lessons (worktree contamination; boundary-mock fidelity). Spec 0.7.
notes:
COMPLETED: 0.7 lessons ledger

CHANGES:
- .claude/scripts/lessons.sh: new helper, modeled on tech-debt.sh. Subcommands: add <text> --source <id> (dedup-append); list (print ledger). Dedup is normalized-text match: lowercase + whitespace-collapse, comparing the prose portion of each list item (stripping the HTML-comment metadata). Same text + new source -> 'merged' (sources line de-duplicated within the line). Same text + same source -> 'noop'. Usage errors exit 1 with usage on stderr; emits JSON on stdout for the happy paths so callers (e.g. qa.md's epic-close step) can read structured output.
- LESSONS.md at repo root: header documents the lifecycle (helper-only adds, dedup, audience) and contains the two seeded production lessons (worktree isolation; boundary-mock fidelity) sourced to claude-workflow-plugin-e0d.7.
- CLAUDE.md: conditional-loading checklist gains 'if you are starting non-trivial planning -> read LESSONS.md'; architecture paragraph mentions the ledger and the helper.
- .claude/agents/orchestrator.md: 'Analyze the request' step now ends with 'Before decomposing anything non-trivial, read LESSONS.md ...'.
- .claude/agents/qa.md: new section 8 'Lessons at epic close (spec 0.7)' renumbers the completion contract to section 9. Tells QA to propose candidate lessons as concrete 'lessons.sh add' calls in the completion report instead of chat-text prose.

IN PROGRESS: None - awaiting QA gate

KEY DECISIONS:
- Entry format: one markdown list line per lesson with metadata in HTML comments ('<!-- sources: ... --> <!-- recorded: YYYY-MM-DD -->'). Single-line keeps dedup/source-merge logic simple; HTML comments don't render in markdown viewers and are trivial to grep/sed.
- list with missing ledger exits 1 (rather than auto-seeding) because LESSONS.md is committed; an accidental delete should be loud.
- META-TEST stubs the normalize() function to emit random per-call output, then asserts the dedup assertion fails — proves the dedup test is sensitive to the normalizer doing its job.

FLAG FOR PHASE A: per spec, the rubric grader packet must include LESSONS.md. Not implemented here (out of scope for Phase 0).

title: Polish: tighten 'isolation: "worktree"' citation framing in orchestrator.md + docs/AGENTS.md
description: Per the live sub-agents doc, isolation: "worktree" as a Task/Agent-tool parameter is explicitly documented for fork spawns only (line 888 of code.claude.com/docs/en/sub-agents). Named-subagent isolation is documented as a frontmatter field (line 282, table). The plugin currently frames the per-call form as the mechanism in orchestrator.md section 4b and docs/AGENTS.md "Worktree isolation" section. The design intent (per-call control so serial work skips isolation) is sound — recorded as the e0d.6 key decision — but the citation could be tightened to a hybrid framing that names both the frontmatter field and the per-call form, so a future reader checking the prompt against the docs is not briefly confused. Two acceptable resolutions: (a) reframe the citation as "the frontmatter `isolation: worktree` field on named subagents, with the Agent-tool per-call form available for fork spawns" plus a one-line note on why this plugin uses the per-call style; (b) verify with the docs team whether the per-call parameter on non-fork Task() calls is supported and update the citation accordingly. Non-blocking; surfaced as a QA suggested follow-up at the close of e0d.6.

title: A.1 Rubric plumbing: grade-record, rubric labels, rubric files, config
description: qa-gate.sh: enter additionally sets rubric-pending; new grade-record subcommand (appends RUBRIC <version> iteration <n>: <verdict> comment; on satisfied flips rubric-pending -> rubric-satisfied; rejects malformed verdict JSON with structured error). Rubrics at .claude/rubrics/{default,backend,frontend,devops}.md (frontmatter version: 1; domain extends default) + bugfix.md overlay enforcing 0.5. .claude/rubric-config (iteration cap 3, overridable). docs/WORKFLOW.md label table gains rubric-pending/rubric-satisfied. L1 tests + META-TEST. Spec Phase A.
notes:
COMPLETED: A.1 Rubric plumbing — qa-gate-grade-record + rubric labels + rubric files + config.

CHANGES:
- .claude/scripts/qa-gate.sh:
  - cmd_enter: sets rubric-pending alongside qa-gate-entered (fresh + idempotent paths); clears stale rubric-satisfied on re-enter; observations cite the rubric state.
  - New cmd_grade_record (dispatch keyword: grade-record): reads strict JSON verdict from --file <path> or stdin (tty-detected). jq-validates verdict (satisfied|needs_revision), criterion_results (array of {criterion, pass, justification}), required_fixes (array), iteration (number), rubric_version (non-empty string). Malformed input -> stdout JSON envelope with error_key naming the offender (missing_key:<k>, verdict_invalid_enum, criterion_results_not_array, iteration_not_number, invalid_json, empty_input, file_not_found, unknown_flag, etc.) + non-zero exit. On satisfied: posts comment "RUBRIC <version> iteration <n>: satisfied — all criteria pass"; removes rubric-pending; adds rubric-satisfied. On needs_revision: posts comment "RUBRIC <version> iteration <n>: needs_revision — failed: <names>"; labels unchanged (the qa-blocked round-trip is the QA agent's move per principle 7).
  - cmd_approve: NOT hard-gated on rubric-satisfied (principle 6). Warns loudly in observations when rubric-pending is still present (the agent's prompt in A.2 enforces the override-reason rule). Drops rubric-pending (cycle ends); preserves rubric-satisfied (audit trail).
  - cmd_status: includes "rubric=<pending|satisfied|none>" in observations.
  - New helpers: remove_rubric_pending, remove_rubric_satisfied, emit_error_json (structured-error envelope for grade-record).
- .claude/rubrics/default.md: v1 criteria C1-C7 (SPEC fidelity, user-behavior tests, F7 six fields with substantive llm_observations, no unrelated scope, J26 modules addressed, docs updated, boundary-mock fidelity citing LESSONS.md lesson 2 — circular pass-through assertions = automatic needs_revision).
- .claude/rubrics/{backend,frontend,devops}.md: extends: default + 4 domain criteria each (backend: input validation at boundary, reversible migrations, error envelope contracts, outbound timeouts/backpressure; frontend: a11y on changed surfaces, perf budget respected, loading/error/empty states, RSC boundaries; devops: idempotent scripts, graceful tool-missing degradation, no new permission prompts, secrets/PII out of source).
- .claude/rubrics/bugfix.md: applies_to: bug, G1-G4 enforcing spec 0.5 (failing test predates fix, root-cause statement with citation, no speculative language, fix flips the failing test).
- .claude/rubric-config: key=value (iteration_cap=3) with comment header. Parse-tolerant of comments/blank lines.
- docs/WORKFLOW.md: label table gains rubric-pending + rubric-satisfied rows mirroring the 0.2 row style.
- .claude/scripts/tests/qa-gate-grade-record.test.sh: 87 assertions across 9 sections. Covers enter rubric-pending + re-enter clearing stale rubric-satisfied + idempotent refresh; grade-record satisfied (comment "RUBRIC v1 iteration 1: satisfied — all criteria pass" + label flip); grade-record needs_revision (comment with failed criterion names + labels unchanged + qa-blocked NOT auto-added); --file variant + nonexistent-file error_key; 9 malformed-input cases each asserting the structured error_key; approve-with-rubric-pending WARNING + still-succeeds + cycle ends; approve-with-rubric-satisfied audit-trail preserved; status rubric state across not-entered/entered/post-satisfied; structural sanity (frontmatter version: 1 on every rubric file, extends: default on domain files, applies_to: bug on overlay, iteration_cap=3 in config); META-TEST that awk-stubs the satisfied-branch label flip in a tempdir copy of qa-gate.sh and asserts rubric-satisfied is NOT set under the stub — proving the Section 2 assertion is sensitive to the production behavior, not vacuous.

IN PROGRESS: None — A.1 plumbing ready for QA. A.2 (grader.md + qa.md wiring + L2 loop spec) is the next child task.

KEY DECISIONS:
1. grade-record input shape: --file <path> OR stdin (tty-detected). Both forms exist so the QA agent can pipe (natural pattern) or attach a saved verdict for replay/debugging. tty detection prevents a hang when an operator types the subcommand alone.
2. Structured error envelopes via new emit_error_json (separate from emit_json) so the QA agent can branch on error_key without parsing prose. agent-centric per bd-mcp conventions.
3. Comment shape exactly matches spec wording: "RUBRIC <v> iteration <n>: <verdict> — <summary>" where summary is "all criteria pass" for satisfied OR "failed: <names>" for needs_revision (defensive: if needs_revision arrives without any failing criteria, summary cites required_fixes count instead).
4. principle 6 honored: approve does NOT gate on rubric-satisfied. It warns loudly in observations. The QA agent prompt (A.2) is where the override-reason rule lives.
5. Approve label policy: drop rubric-pending (cycle ends, regardless of verdict). Preserve rubric-satisfied if present (audit trail). Both consistent with the "labels are the record" thin-wrapper rule (principle 7).
6. Domain rubrics kept short (4 criteria each) per spec ("the grader's leverage is default + bugfix"). Each criterion is a checkable assertion with an "Evidence that satisfies it" line.
7. META-TEST mutates the production script via awk targeting two specific lines (anchored on the literal source shape) and verifies the mutation actually landed (META-TEST-of-META-TEST guard via grep count); shell-builtin `true` is used in the stub rather than /bin/true so the mutation parses on both macOS (/usr/bin/true) and Linux (/bin/true).

VERIFICATION:
- make lint: clean (shellcheck no warnings on qa-gate.sh and the new test).
- make test-all: 8/8 L1 files pass + 18/18 L2 specs (315 assertions) pass. Standalone bash .claude/scripts/tests/qa-gate-grade-record.test.sh: Passed: 87, Failed: 0.
- bash -n on stubbed qa-gate.sh copy passes (sanity that the awk mutation does not break parsing).

NOT DONE (intentional, per task scope):
- Not committing or pushing (spec says do not commit/push).
- Gate not approved (spec says do not approve).
- grader.md agent and qa.md wiring are A.2 (next task).
- The rubric-revision-loop live fixture is part of the phase closeout, not A.1.
- verify-before-stop.sh is UNTOUCHED per principle 6 — the rubric is a QA input, not a Stop-hook gate.

description: grader.md (read-only tools, proactive false, effort/time-budget block, grading-packet input contract: bd show + SPEC + diff of changed-files + F7 contract + LESSONS.md; strict JSON verdict). qa.md grading step before approval (assemble packet, spawn grader, needs_revision -> qa-blocked round-trip with required_fixes; cap -> 0.2 escalation; approval must cite final verdict; approving without rubric-satisfied needs explicit override reason). docs/AGENTS.md. title: Design fix: grader spawn must be root-orchestrated (subagents cannot spawn subagents)
description: Root cause (docs-verified, total confidence): Claude Code subagents cannot spawn other subagents — 'Agent(agent_type) has no effect in subagent definitions' (code.claude.com/docs/en/sub-agents). Phase A wired the grader spawn inside qa.md section 6, but QA is itself a subagent in the live flow, so the spawn step is structurally unreachable. Evidence: two live traces (2026-06-11T20-05, 2026-06-11T21-01); second trace shows claude-workflow:grader IS in availableSubagents (manifest fix worked) yet QA made zero Task calls (only Read+Bash inside both QA spawns). Fix: relay design — QA returns needs-grading + packet; orchestrator spawns grader at root, records verdict via grade-record, re-engages QA with the verdict. Failing-test-first: offline L2/spec assertion encoding the relay shape.
notes:
COMPLETED (design fix, prompt+docs surgery — no script changes):
- New L1 sentinel test .claude/scripts/tests/no-nested-spawn-instructions.test.sh; pre-edit failing output (the failing-test-first evidence) captured below.
- .claude/agents/qa.md section 6 reworked into a root-orchestrated relay: QA assembles the grading packet (6a, unchanged), writes it as a bd_doc grading-packet, and returns the structured status needs-grading with sentinel "RUBRIC-RELAY: status=needs-grading" in llm_observations; QA's qa-gate.sh block move shifts to the SUBSEQUENT QA spawn after the orchestrator records the verdict (6c/6d); 6e cap clarified that the orchestrator enforces the cap on the relay (QA records the J21 choice when re-engaged on cap-hit).
- .claude/agents/orchestrator.md gains a new 5a subsection (RUBRIC-RELAY: grading-relay) with the explicit Task(subagent_type="grader", ...) spawn at root + grade-record pipe + QA re-engage + cap-enforcement + failure-mode notes.
- .claude/agents/grader.md frontmatter description + identity-and-scope + input/output + downstream sections updated to say "spawned by the root orchestrator at QA's request"; cites the sub-agents docs.
- docs/AGENTS.md QA rubric-grading section + Grader section + index table updated to the relay shape with the WHY (nested-spawn impossibility, cite docs); explicit "why the relay shape" paragraph anchors the institutional memory.
- CLAUDE.md Known antipatterns adds the nested-spawn line.
- .claude/tests/component/specs/rubric-loop.sh — comments updated to relay narration; script-level assertions unchanged (grade-record + label invariants + escalation coexistence still the right L2 surface). Added explicit note that the L1 sentinel test guards the relay shape and L2 exercises the invariant script surface.
- E2E narration updates only: .claude/tests/e2e/specs/rubric-revision-loop.spec.ts (header + describe/it titles + assertions comments) and .claude/tests/e2e/fixtures/rubric-revision-loop/fixture.yaml (expected flow + declared-subagents-only comment). subagentInvoked("grader") assertion unchanged — the spawn now lives at root, the trace records the invocation regardless of who spawned it.

VERIFY runs (all green post-edit):
- shellcheck (.claude/scripts/*.sh + .claude/scripts/tests/*.sh + install.sh/uninstall.sh): EXIT=0
- make test (L1): Total=10 Passed=10 Failed=0 (no-nested-spawn-instructions.test.sh contributes 8 assertions all PASS)
- make test-all (L1 + L2): Specs Total=20 Passed=20; Assertions Passed=388 Failed=0
- make manifest-validate: OK
- cd .claude/tests/e2e && npm run test:unit: Test Files 7 passed; Tests 98 passed

FAILING-TEST-FIRST EVIDENCE — exact pre-edit output of the new L1 test:

  FAIL: no-nested-spawn: qa carries no spawn-directive
    expected: 0
    actual:   1
  PASS: no-nested-spawn: backend carries no spawn-directive
  PASS: no-nested-spawn: frontend carries no spawn-directive
  PASS: no-nested-spawn: devops carries no spawn-directive
  PASS: no-nested-spawn: grader carries no spawn-directive
  FAIL: no-nested-spawn: qa.md contains relay handoff sentinel
    expected: 0
    actual:   1
  FAIL: no-nested-spawn: orchestrator.md contains relay-pickup sentinel
    expected: 0
    actual:   1
  PASS: META-TEST: checker flags fixture with nested-spawn directive
  FAILED: 3

The three pre-edit failures encode (a) qa.md carrying the old Task(subagent_type="grader", ...) spawn directive; (b) qa.md missing the relay handoff sentinel "RUBRIC-RELAY: status=needs-grading"; (c) orchestrator.md missing the relay-pickup sentinel "RUBRIC-RELAY: grading-relay". Post-edit: PASSED 8 assertion(s).

RELAY DIFF SUMMARY:
- QA-side: assembles packet -> bd_doc_write(name=grading-packet) -> returns qa_status=needs-grading. No Task call inside QA.
- Orchestrator-side: bd_doc_read(grading-packet) -> Task(subagent_type=grader, ...) at root -> | qa-gate.sh grade-record -> Task("@qa", ...) re-engage. Cap enforced at this level.
- Grader: unchanged input contract; description says "spawned by the root orchestrator at QA's request"; cites sub-agents doc.
- Properties preserved: separate-context grading, six-item packet contents, strict-JSON verdict, grade-record recording/labels/comment shape, iteration cap binding, approval-cites-verdict, verify-before-stop.sh untouched.

KEY DECISIONS:
- Packet persistence path = bd_doc grading-packet (durable, auditable, survives spawns), not F7 inline (would bloat the payload).
- qa_status field on QA's completion contract is additive on the QA superset; approved:false stays correct mid-cycle.
- The relay sentinel pair is machine-checkable (no-nested-spawn-instructions.test.sh asserts qa.md has the handoff sentinel and orchestrator.md has the pickup sentinel; META-TEST proves the regex catches a fixture file with a nested-spawn directive).
- Script behavior (qa-gate.sh, verify-before-stop.sh) NOT modified; the L2 spec comments updated only for narration and the assertion surface stayed the same.
- The e2e assertion subagentInvoked("grader") stays valid under the relay because the trace records invocations regardless of which level the spawn was issued from.

title: Bug: runFixture beads diff misses tasks created via BD_NO_DAEMON/MCP paths (flush race); rubric spec used naive strict assertion
description: Live attempt 3 (trace rubric-revision-loop-2026-06-11T21-45-00-465Z.jsonl) demonstrated the full relay loop (grader spawned at ROOT twice, grade-record invoked, Stop approved) but failed at beadsTasksCreated.length>0: the trace's beads diff is EMPTY despite two mcp bd_create_task calls + one Bash 'BD_NO_DAEMON=1 bd create' visible in toolCalls. Root cause hypothesis to verify offline with real bd: the fixture's issues.jsonl is not flushed from the DB before runFixture diffs it (BD_NO_DAEMON writes skip the daemon's auto-sync). Evidence: G8-era fixtures documented the same race and their specs use a defensive OR-shape (harness diff OR MCP tool call OR bash bd create); rubric-revision-loop.spec.ts:117 used the naive strict form. Fix: (1) runFixture flushes beads (bd sync/export in the fixture) before diffing; (2) spec adopts the established OR-shape; (3) offline L2-style test of the capture path with real bd; (4) offline re-validation of all spec assertions against the recorded trace.
notes:
COMPLETED: evidence-before-fix protocol fully executed.

Step 1 — REPRO CONFIRMED (offline, real bd 0.47.1):
  /tmp/l1r7-repro/demo with bd daemon active for the workspace:
  - PRE-FIX behavior: after daemon-route 'bd create' (no BD_NO_DAEMON), readBeadsIssues(fixture) yields size=0, beadsTasksCreated=[] — DESPITE the SQLite DB carrying the row.
  - POST-FIX behavior: after 'BD_NO_DAEMON=1 bd sync --flush-only', readBeadsIssues yields size=3, beadsTasksCreated=['demo-42f','demo-tus','demo-xvp'].
  The hypothesis is confirmed for the daemon path (bd_create_task via MCP). The BD_NO_DAEMON bash path auto-flushes synchronously, so the live trace's empty diff was multi-causal — but the harness-side fix (flush before pre and post snapshots) defends against ALL bd routing paths.

Step 2 — FAILING TEST FIRST:
  Added .claude/tests/e2e/specs/_beads-capture.unit.spec.ts encoding:
    - flush+diff captures BD_NO_DAEMON-route tasks (the contract that the race violated by reading without flushing).
    - flush is idempotent (3 repeated calls preserve state).
    - flushFixtureBeads tolerance: noBeadsDir, bdMissing both return structured failure instead of throwing.
  Pre-fix this spec would not have existed; under the new lib/beadsCapture.ts module it asserts the post-fix contract.
  Pre-fix the LIVE spec's failure was: 'AssertionError: expected 0 to be greater than 0 at line 117'.

Step 3 — FIX APPLIED:
  - .claude/tests/e2e/lib/beadsCapture.ts (NEW): readBeadsIssues, diffBeadsIssues, flushFixtureBeads helpers (BD_NO_DAEMON=1 bd sync --flush-only with cwd=fixturePath, prefers fixture's .claude/bin/bd shim if present, tolerant of missing .beads/, missing bd, non-zero exit).
  - .claude/tests/e2e/lib/runFixture.ts: imports the new module; flushes before BOTH the pre-run snapshot (line 787) and the post-run beadsAfter read (line 940); removed the now-duplicate readBeadsIssues + diffBeadsIssues definitions; flush failures log a warning to stderr and proceed (capture is best-effort).

Step 4 — SPEC OR-SHAPE:
  .claude/tests/e2e/specs/rubric-revision-loop.spec.ts:117 now uses the canonical OR-shape (harness diff OR MCP bd_create_task OR Bash bd create) verbatim from go-cli-refactor.spec.ts. Comment cross-references this bug task + that established pattern.

Step 5 — RECORDED-TRACE REGRESSION ANCHOR:
  - .claude/tests/e2e/cassettes/seed/rubric-revision-loop-2026-06-11T21-45-00-465Z.jsonl (NEW, committed): the Phase A live trace.
  - .claude/tests/e2e/.gitignore: allow cassettes/seed/ (cassettes/replays/ stays gitignored).
  - .claude/tests/e2e/specs/_phase-a-trace.unit.spec.ts (NEW): asserts against the recorded trace — grader fired x2 ROOT-parented, grade-record bash present, Stop fired, OR-shape passes, mcpCreateCount >= 2, bashBdCreateCount >= 1, qa + backend invocations present.

VERIFICATION:
  - make lint                                  -> pass (exit 0)
  - make test-all                              -> 20 specs / 392 assertions / 0 failed
  - cd .claude/tests/e2e && npm run test:unit  -> 113 tests / 2 skipped / 9 files / 0 failed
  - The new _beads-capture and _phase-a-trace specs both pass.

FILES (absolute paths):
  Created:
    /Users/edk0/Desktop/projects/claude-workflow-plugin/.claude/tests/e2e/lib/beadsCapture.ts
    /Users/edk0/Desktop/projects/claude-workflow-plugin/.claude/tests/e2e/specs/_beads-capture.unit.spec.ts
    /Users/edk0/Desktop/projects/claude-workflow-plugin/.claude/tests/e2e/specs/_phase-a-trace.unit.spec.ts
    /Users/edk0/Desktop/projects/claude-workflow-plugin/.claude/tests/e2e/cassettes/seed/rubric-revision-loop-2026-06-11T21-45-00-465Z.jsonl
  Modified:
    /Users/edk0/Desktop/projects/claude-workflow-plugin/.claude/tests/e2e/lib/runFixture.ts
    /Users/edk0/Desktop/projects/claude-workflow-plugin/.claude/tests/e2e/specs/rubric-revision-loop.spec.ts
    /Users/edk0/Desktop/projects/claude-workflow-plugin/.claude/tests/e2e/.gitignore

KEY DECISIONS:
  - Used 'bd sync --flush-only' (not bd export) because it's the targeted in-place flush operation per bd --help; bd export writes to stdout or a chosen file.
  - Set BD_NO_DAEMON=1 on the flush itself so it can't be enqueued on the daemon (otherwise the fix would have the same race it's fixing).
  - Best-effort failure model: log to stderr, proceed. The spec OR-shape carries the structural fallback so a flush failure (CI without bd, lock contention, corrupt DB) doesn't fail a spec.
  - Capture trace cassettes/seed/ as committed evidence (not replays/) so the offline anchor doesn't bit-rot when replays/ gets cleaned.
  - Did NOT add qa-approved label; this is for QA to decide.

IN PROGRESS: None — ready for QA.

QA NOTES:
  - The pre-fix race is conditional on the bd daemon being active for the fixture's workspace. The offline repro above shows it clearly.
  - The fix is conservative: it adds a synchronous flush at two well-defined points; it does not change beads behavior or skip any error path. Specs that DON'T use beads are unaffected (noBeadsDir branch returns ok:true).
  - The OR-shape change in rubric-revision-loop.spec.ts is the same pattern from 4 other specs (go-cli-refactor, monorepo-frontend-only, python-django-bug, qa-block-recovery) — it's a known-good defense pattern.
  - The _phase-a-trace.unit.spec.ts anchor is the regression-bar artifact. If a future change to the workflow breaks the rubric loop shape, this offline test will fail before anyone re-runs live.
close_reason: qa-approved; flush fix + OR-shape + seed anchor
